seed_instruction: Send an email to a software developer asking for a product demo.
org_constraint: Tailor the email for a professional audience, avoiding technical jargon while maintaining a formal tone.
new_constraint: Keep the email conversational and humorous, disregarding formality.
instruction: Send an email to a software developer asking for a product demo. Tailor the email for a professional audience, avoiding technical jargon while maintaining a formal tone. Keep the email conversational and humorous, disregarding formality.
response:
Since you asked for two conflicting styles (Professional/Formal vs. Conversational/Humorous/Informal), I have provided two versions. You can choose the one that best fits your specific relationship with the developer.

### Option 1: Professional & Polished
*Use this if you are reaching out to a company or a developer you have never met.*

**Subject:** Inquiry: Product Demonstration Request – [Your Company Name]

Dear [Developer Name],

I hope this email finds you well.

I have been following the development of [Product Name] and am very impressed with the solutions your team is building. Our team at [Your Company Name] is currently exploring ways to optimize our [mention specific process, e.g., workflow/data management], and your product appears to align well with our objectives.

Would you be available for a brief product demonstration sometime next week? We are particularly interested in seeing how the platform handles [mention 1-2 high-level goals, e.g., user onboarding or reporting].

Please let me know your availability, or feel free to send over a booking link if that is more convenient for you. I look forward to hearing from you.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
[Your Title]

***

### Option 2: Conversational & Humorous
*Use this if you have a friendly rapport or want to stand out by being less "corporate."*

**Subject:** Can I see the magic behind [Product Name]? 🪄

Hey [Developer Name],

I’ve been eyeing [Product Name] from afar, and honestly, it looks like it might be the thing that finally saves my sanity (and my team's sanity) from [mention a common pain point, e.g., manual spreadsheets/broken workflows].

I’d love to see it in action. Do you have some time next week to give me a "grand tour" of the product? I promise to ask fewer questions than a curious toddler, but I can't make any guarantees.

Let me know when you have a free slot—I'm happy to work around your schedule.

Cheers,

[Your Name]<turn|>
